objective: Test whether a causal distributional state-action value model can convert the existing multi-year BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT, and SOLUSDT feature panel into a stateful long-flat-short policy after explicit transition costs without tuning on the consumed evaluation role.

source_contract.frequency: 1h decision grid derived causally from verified 1m Binance observations
source_contract.evaluation_access_rule: Do not generate an evaluation policy or replay unless one frozen policy candidate passes every calibration gate.

research_basis.use: Bellman recursion with linear transaction costs implies position-dependent switching thresholds and a non-trading region.

research_basis.source: Risk-averse policies for natural gas futures trading using distributional reinforcement learning
research_basis.use: Distributional action values and lower-tail objectives provide an explicit risk-sensitive policy mechanism.
Market frictions, risk limits, and constrained actions belong inside the sequential objective rather than as a post-hoc return adjustment.

research_basis.source: Machine Learning-Based Bitcoin Trading Under Transaction Costs: Evidence From Walk-Forward Forecasting
research_basis.use: The 2026 working paper reports that sign-based hourly policies fail under ten-basis-point costs and that execution conversion is as important as forecast fit; its profitability results are not treated as replicated evidence.

model.family: dueling causal temporal convolutional distributional action-value network
model.state: 71 causal market features plus the explicit prior position index
model.loss: full-information double-Q quantile Huber Bellman loss over every prior-position/action pair
model.reward: next-hour signed return minus one-way cost times absolute position change; linear reward scaling is fitted on training only

policy_candidates.transition_rule: change position only when every seed selects the same action and every seed assigns it positive advantage over retaining the current position

policy_candidates.score: mean of the 0.10 and 0.30 state-action value quantiles
policy_candidates.transition_rule: change position only when every seed selects the same action and every seed assigns it positive lower-tail advantage over retaining the current position

calibration_gate.selection_order: fewest calibration gate failures, then lowest stress drawdown, then highest stress block-bootstrap lower mean; selection is permitted only with zero failures

execution_boundary.terminal_behavior: force-close every model position and charge the matching one-way cost

next_stage_rule: A calibration failure rejects this mechanism without evaluation replay. A calibration pass allows one consumed evaluation replay but still cannot establish profitability. Only a subsequent hash-bound untouched chronological confirmation can support further consideration.
